steps.title: Identify the task
steps.content: The student wants to "emphasize a similarity between the two works." The notes show one relevant shared trait: both Erasure and Home Grown feature discarded objects. The correct choice must name both works and highlight that shared feature.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Erasure (2008) uses discarded objects such as audiocassette tapes and magnets; Home Grown (2009), however, includes pushpins, plastic plates and forks, and wood.': The contrast word "however" signals a difference, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Tubbs's work, which often features discarded objects, has been shown both within the United States and abroad.': Never mentions Erasure or Home Grown by name, so it fails to connect the two works. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Like many of Tubbs's sculptures, both Erasure and Home Grown include discarded objects: Erasure uses audiocassette tapes, and Home Grown uses plastic forks.': Names both works, identifies the shared trait (discarded objects), and gives a specific example from each. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Tubbs completed Erasure in 2008 and Home Grown in 2009.': Mentions only completion dates, not any shared feature of the works. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
